title: Centralizing Fine-Grained Decisions
description:
## Getting Access Logic Out of the App

Your API has grown a tangle of hardcoded `if (hasRole(...))` checks. Product wants access rules to change frequently and depend on more than roles, and security wants the rules managed **centrally** rather than redeployed with the app each time.

Which Keycloak capability is designed for this?

options.text: **Keycloak Authorization Services**: turn the confidential client into a resource server, model **Resources** and **Scopes**, write **Policies**, and combine them into **Permissions**; the app delegates the decision to Keycloak (request an RPT or use the Policy Enforcer)
Add more **realm roles** and keep enforcing them with role checks in the application code
Switch the client's protocol to **SAML** — SAML assertions natively carry permission decisions
Store every rule as a custom claim via protocol mappers and let the app parse them at runtime

explanation: **Authorization Services** is exactly the feature for externalizing fine-grained, condition-based access control. A confidential client becomes a **resource server**; you define **Resources** (protected things) and **Scopes** (actions), author reusable **Policies** (role, user, group, time, regex, JS, aggregate...), and bind them via **Permissions**. The app stops hardcoding decisions and instead delegates — requesting a **Requesting Party Token (RPT)** at the token endpoint (UMA grant) or letting the **Policy Enforcer** intercept requests. Rules then change in Keycloak without redeploying. (b) keeps the problem — still role-only and still enforced in code. (c) is wrong — SAML is an authentication protocol, not an authorization-decision engine. (d) just moves the tangle into claims; the app still hardcodes the evaluation logic instead of delegating it.

hints.title: Resource server + RPT
hints.body: Enable 'Authorization' on a confidential client to expose the Authorization tab (Resources, Scopes, Policies, Permissions). At runtime the client exchanges its access token for an RPT that encodes the granted permissions.
